name: MAXIMUM_LENGTH_NAME_
description: This is a maximum length description to test the upper boundary of the validation constraints. It contains exactly six hundred characters to ensure that the document logic handles the strict limit correctly without triggering an overflow error. We are filling this space with meaningful text to simulate a real-world complex machine definition where technical details require significant elaboration. Every character counts toward the final limit of six hundred, covering multiple sentences and providing enough context for the validator to process this large block of text efficiently.

elements.name: MAX_ELEM_NAME_LIMIT
elements.description: This is another maximum length description but for an internal element. It also reaches the six hundred character limit to verify that nested components are validated with the same rigor as the root document. Sound synthesis machines often require highly detailed technical documentation to explain signal flow, modulation matrices, and granular processing parameters. By filling this description to the absolute maximum, we can guarantee that the JSON parser and the Langium validator are compatible with large string payloads without performance degradation or unexpected truncation issues.
elements.params: This parameters field is also at its maximum capacity of six hundred characters. It usually contains comma-separated key-value pairs representing oscillator frequencies, filter cutoffs, resonance values, LFO rates, and modulation depths. In this edge case test, we ensure that the entire parameter string is preserved and validated correctly. The grammar specifies that this field is mandatory and must contain between ten and six hundred characters. We are currently utilizing every single byte available to push the boundaries of the model and verify the robustness of our CIM implementation.

externalInput.description: This is a maximum length description for the external input port of the machine. It is limited to six hundred characters as well, allowing sound designers to specify exactly what kind of signal is expected at this stage. Whether it is a sidechain trigger, a carrier signal for a vocoder, or a modulation source for an FM operator, this field provides the necessary metadata for the system to understand connectivity requirements. Testing this limit ensures that the UI and the backend can handle long strings without breaking the layout or failing to persist the data to the database.

externalOutput.description: Finally, this is the maximum length description for the external output port. Like all other description fields in this DSL, it allows for a detailed explanation of the output signal characteristics, such as frequency range, signal-to-noise ratio, and intended destination. By validating this final field at its six hundred character limit, we complete the stress test for the entire machine model. This ensures that every part of the JSON structure that supports large text blocks is fully functional and compliant with the architectural constraints defined in the Langium grammar file.

sendTo.description: Maximum length description for the sendTo connection object. This field also supports up to six hundred characters to explain the routing logic between internal elements. It is crucial for complex feedback loops and modulation chains where the purpose of a connection might not be immediately obvious. By stretching this field to its limit, we confirm that the link between nodes and their routing metadata is stable and that the validator correctly identifies the boundary of valid string input.
